## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so I'm going to quickly take you into an ad account and show you interest targeting, behavioral and demographic targeting and how to use these. But before I do, I quickly want to explain to you how this type of targeting works. Now remember this is for cold audiences, so people that have never, haven't necessarily interacted or engaged with you before. Cold targeting works by going after people that have expressed or engaged with content that is related to what you are targeting. So for example, let's say you mentioned the word Greece in a post one time or you liked a Facebook post that was about Greece or you shared a post that had something about Greece in it. Well, this would officially bucket you as someone who was engaged with or potentially interested in Greece. Now Facebook collects this information in a few different ways. So it collects it through online behavior. So as you all know, there's Facebook pixels all across the web. And with the new metadata that it has on that pixel, it's able to send a lot more information back into Facebook in terms of what people are doing, what they're searching for, what they're interested in. It's also looking at your own Facebook behavior. So what you're engaging with on your newsfeed, what you're interacting with, what you're having conversations around. It's listening to you through messenger. So if you're having conversations with friends around Greece or you spoke to someone about Greece, it's also reading your emails on your phone. So that's part of its terms of service when you download the app to your phone. So it will see that you received a booking confirmation for flights to Greece, for example. And it also taps into third-party data such as Axiom. So it knows if you own a home. It knows if you're a single parent. It knows how much money you're making every year. It knows all of this because of all these data points that it's able to tap into. And so when you engage with anything about Greece, you potentially bucket yourself as an interest. Facebook, however, will know who is more inclined to take action for whatever you are optimizing for. So they'll know essentially who's further down in the marketing funnel and who is just sort of randomly engaging with something. And that's part of the EA expected action in the algorithm. Remember that part? Well, that's what Facebook taps into, and that's sort of how it does it. So before I take you into an actual ad account, a couple of important things here with interest-based

## [03:02 - 06:05]

targeting. Continuity is a biggie. And this is something we're finding that Facebook really, really loves right now. And it makes sense because it's all about relevance. So here's what we're finding. Let's say you want to target the interest, Greece. Maybe you're selling t-shirts for people that are about to travel to Greece or that have traveled to Greece, whatever that might be. So you're targeting the interest, Greece. What you want to do is you want to make sure that your ad copy also has the word Greece in it, not only that, but that your landing page, your sales page, has Greece in it as well. And having continuity of the same word across the targeting, the ad copy, and the landing page is really, really going to help you drop that CPM and increase the relevance in your ad. So here's where we're finding people getting really incredible results like cost per leads or cost per conversions because of this continuity. So this is an important part. Now let's go check out an ad account, and I'll show you some of the options that you have under there. Okay, so targeting takes place at the ad set level, remember. So I'm at the ad set level now, and I can either set locations and age and genders. Now there's this one tab here for languages. Typically we don't fill this out unless there's a very specific language we want to go after. So perhaps we're specifically targeting Spanish speaking individuals or Mandarin speaking individuals, but generally speaking, there's absolutely no need to include English here. If your ad copy is in English and your ad account and your page is in English, Facebook already knows that you're trying to go after English speaking individuals. Now here's where the magic happens for interest, behavioral, and demographic targeting. So under here is where we can add the interest that we're after. So in this example, let's say Greece. So we had Greece in there, and you'll see it comes up as an interest. It also comes up as an employer, so there's a company that's called Greece, or maybe this is the government. There's schools, ancient Greece as interest, lots of different options here. Let's go and just select Greece for a moment. And you'll see here on the right hand side, Facebook's going to show you the potential reach and the audience size. Now remember to work backwards with your client objectives to find out what kind of audience size you need to be working with. This may be too small for you, depending on what objectives you have. Now some of the other options that we have here, if you just click on browse, you'll

see this little drop down, and so you'll see demographics, interests, behaviors. Now one of the things that we see getting clicked a lot here under digital activities is Facebook payments. Now just be aware that this is actually targeting a lot of gamers for the moment. And you've got things like travel as well. What kind of travelers are they? Are they business travelers, commuters? Are you going after nine to fivers? This could be a great one, right? For example, if you're going after marketers potentially targeting Hootsuite or Sprout Social or any tools, marketing tools that they use, Stripe, PayPal, Google AdWords, what are the conferences that they go to?
